Why SOAC

More Than a Club. A Community.

SOAC gives you a place to grow, compete, create, and serve — with real rewards for real effort.

🎓

Learn Beyond Class

Build skills — from coding Android apps to leading NCC drills — that employers actually value.

🤝

Find Your People

40 clubs across 6 categories. There is a community for every student.

🏆

Real Rewards

Earn XP, SOAC Coins and badges. Top 3 coin holders per club get free annual re-registration.

📅

Structured Growth

Weekly schedules, assigned tasks, event calendars — so you always know your next step.

💬

Stay Connected

Group chats, DMs, club news feeds and SOAC updates — all in one place.

🌎

Campus Legacy

The Wall of Fame is permanent. Your contributions recorded forever at RKU.

One Portal.

Simple Process

How to Join a Club

1

Sign Up

Create your account with your RKU student email in under 2 minutes.

2

Browse Clubs

Explore all 40 clubs across technology, sports, culture, health, and community.

3

Request to Join

Submit a join request. Your coordinator reviews and approves your membership.

4

Attend & Earn

Show up, earn XP and SOAC Coins. Climb the leaderboard.

About SOAC

Student Organizations
Advisory Council

The official body governing all student-run organizations at RKU.

🏛 University Recognised 📋 40 Active Clubs 🎓 Est. 2019 🏆 50+ Events / Year
40+
Clubs
What is SOAC

Building Leaders
One Club at a Time

RKU believes that student-led organizations enhance education by providing opportunities beyond the curriculum for personal development, leadership, and growth.

SOAC assists students in developing organizations, planning events, provides financial guidance, and authorizes use of University resources.

🎓
Beyond the Classroom
Interpersonal, organizational and leadership skills in a real environment.
🏛
University Recognised
Every SOAC club has formal recognition, resources and advisory support.
🏆
Rewarded Participation
XP, Coins, badges, and free re-registration for top performers each year.
Objectives

What SOAC Stands For

📚

Academic & Cultural Growth

Enhancement of academic, cultural, recreational, social and sports aspects of student life.

🧭

Skill & Organisation

Increase knowledge and skill in functioning within an organization.

🌿

Personal Development

Develop a positive attitude toward yourself, your peers, and the university community.

🌎

Community Responsibility

Promote awareness through conferences, activities and community service.

Consultation Hierarchy

Who to Approach at SOAC

Every student has a clear path to get support. Here's the hierarchy for one club — the same structure applies across all 40 SOAC clubs.

🏆
Head of SOAC
Highest Authority
Final escalation point for all club-related matters. Oversees all 40 clubs and SOAC operations at RKU.
📍 Admin Block, Room 101
📋
Club Coordinator
e.g. Android Development Club
Your primary contact for club activities, event planning, attendance, task assignments and day-to-day club operations.
Events Attendance Tasks Join Requests
💲
Club Treasurer
Finance & Budget
Manages club funds, budgets for events, handles registrations fees and financial records for the club.
Fees Budgets Reimbursements
👥
Co-Coordinator
Support & Operations
Assists the main coordinator, handles member onboarding, manages communication and supports club events.
Onboarding Communication
🎓
You — The Student
Start here. For any club query, approach your Co-Coordinator first, then Coordinator, then Treasurer for financial matters, and escalate to Head of SOAC if needed.
💡
Escalation Guide
Always start with your Co-Coordinator for day-to-day issues. If unresolved within 48 hours, escalate to your Club Coordinator. Financial matters go directly to the Treasurer. For policy disputes or formal complaints, approach the Head of SOAC at Admin Block, Room 101 during office hours (Mon–Fri, 9 AM – 5 PM).

Propose a New Club
Reviewed by SOAC board · 7 working days turnaround · All fields marked * are required
1
Applicant
2
Advisor & Team
3
Club Details
4
Activity Plan
5
Review & Submit
Part A — Applicant Information
Tell us about the proposed organization and the lead applicant.
Academic / Cultural & Recreational / Social & Humanitarian / Sports
Independent (new) or Extension of existing organization
Primary Student Applicant
Part B — Advisor & Leadership Team
Every SOAC club requires a Faculty Advisor. All three leadership roles are mandatory.
🎓 Primary Faculty Advisor
Upload your advisor's signed consent letter to Google Drive and paste the link
👥 Coordinator
👥 Convener
👥 Treasurer
Part C — Club Details & Funding
Define your club's objectives, membership model, and how it will be funded.
Minimum 3 objectives. Each objective should be specific and achievable.
Membership Criteria *
Source of Funding
Membership Fees
Institute / Org Sponsorship
📄 Board Presentation Checklist
✅ Organization name & applicant details
✅ 3+ clear objectives
✅ Membership criteria & fee structure
✅ Membership & procedural guidelines
✅ Full leadership team (Coordinator, Convener, Treasurer)
✅ Event overview & estimated budget
Activity Planner
Based on the SOAC Activity Planner format. List any relevant past activities and your planned future events.
Past Activities / Events (if any)

Brief Description

Minimum 1 required

📄 SOAC Board Review Presentation Template
Download and use this template for your board presentation. Follow the exact format.
🔒 What happens after you submit?
1. SOAC board reviews your application within 7 working days.
2. If shortlisted, you’ll be invited to present using the downloaded PPT template.
3. Present objectives, membership plan, leadership team, events & budget estimate.
4. If approved, formal registration begins at Admin Block, Room 101.
Review Your Application
Check all details carefully. Click Edit on any section to go back and make changes before submitting.
🎉
Application Submitted!
Your club proposal for has been sent to the SOAC board. You’ll hear back within 7 working days at your registered email.
Next Steps
📄 Download & prepare your board presentation PPT
👥 Confirm your leadership team is ready
📅 Keep faculty advisor consent letter handy
📍 Board review held at Admin Block, Room 101
Step 1 of 4
Club Proposal — Full Review

Actions are applied immediately and visible to the applicant on their next visit.
